Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine and potassium, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, supporting possible nephropathy. HbA1c is low, but urine glucose is present, which may indicate glycosuria unrelated to hyperglycemia. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying renal and metabolic disorders.
